summary: Energetic second set with China>Rider; first half feels average
blurb: The show delivers a strong second set highlighted by China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and a lively Hey Jude finale. Jerry’s voice is a bit scratchy but spirited, and Brent adds flair on keyboards. Some cuts in the first set (Let It Grow) keep the overall flow average.
review: On March 17, 1988 at the Henry J. Kaiser Convention Center the Dead offered a typical two‑set layout with a relaxed opener that never quite reaches its peak, but the second half kicks into high gear. The China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue shines, followed by an Estimated Prophet featuring a memorable wah‑wah bridge and an expansive Eyes of the World jam that rolls straight into a vibrant Space and a rousing Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad. Brent’s hand‑gesturing during the Hey Jude finale adds a playful touch, while Bob Weir’s vocal conviction drives the band forward. Though Jerry’s voice sounds a little scratchy at times and the Let It Grow cut feels short, the overall performance is solid enough to make the night worth revisiting, especially for fans of the second‑set energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Recording analyses show mixed sentiment overall (two mixed, one positive, one negative). No consistent praise or criticism across multiple sources, so a neutral adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard with clear, balanced mix and captures the energetic second set best.

key_highlights: Energetic second set featuring China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and a lively Hey Jude finale
"Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again" moment stands out in the first set
Brent’s hand‑gesturing on keyboards adds visual flair during the Hey Jude finale
Mixed show quality: strong second half but average first half with some cuts (Let It Grow)
song_highlights: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again

band_performance.Jerry: Voice is slightly scratchy yet spirited; delivers a notable wah‑wah bridge on Estimated Prophet and stays lively through the second set.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals have conviction, driving the band forward especially during the high‑energy moments.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work is present throughout; hand‑gesturing during the Hey Jude finale adds extra flair.
band_performance.Mickey: Mardi‑Gras percussionists add extra flavor to the drum segment, enhancing the rhythmic texture.
band_performance.Billy: Drumming stays solid; the added Mardi‑Gras percussive elements complement his groove.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again
